content.text: Control flow directs an agent through decisions: which tool to call next, whether to retry after failure, when to stop iterating, and how to branch on model output. Conditionals and loops are the executable logic layer on top of data and operators.
Control flow statements change which code runs based on conditions or repetition. Core constructs:

The agent loop — observe, reason, act — is implemented with while/for loops and if branches. Retry policies, budget checks, and termination conditions all live here. Off-by-one errors and infinite loops are common failure modes in production agents.
— natural fit for iterating messages, tools, and search results
— encode "run until done or limit reached" agent cycles
— clean dispatch on tool names or structured event types
— many nested if/for blocks hurt readability; extract functions
— needs explicit break or return to avoid runaway agents
— rarely used; surprises readers unfamiliar with it

def handle_event(event: dict) -> None:
    match event.get("type"):
        case "tool_call":
            dispatch_tool(event["name"], event["args"])
        case "message":
            append_message(event["content"])
        case _:
            log_unknown(event)
when iterating a known collection (messages, files, tools)
when termination depends on runtime state (model says done, budget exhausted)
when dispatching on discrete enum-like values (event types, tool names)
for numeric thresholds and compound policy rules
Exponential backoff on rate limits inside a retry loop:
— missing break when model keeps requesting tools
— iterate over a copy or build a new list
fails on legitimate empty outputs; be explicit
— else runs only if loop did not break
Use guard clauses to flatten nested if blocks
Extract loop bodies into named functions when logic grows beyond ~15 lines

Unbounded loops are a denial-of-service risk in server-side agents. Always combine wall-clock timeouts with turn limits. Validate branch conditions so untrusted model output cannot skip safety checks.
: conditionals pick forks, loops repeat until a exit condition,
skips to the next iteration. The agent loop is a while with an inner for over tool calls.
Agent loops are while + break on completion or limits
for iterates collections; match dispatches on structured events
Always bound retries and turns in production agents
Prefer guard clauses and small functions over deep nesting
